title: Shadows of Brimstone: A Quick Look at What Will be in our Next Campaign
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SW7PfbETFx8

Daniel (@dungeondive) lays out the full arsenal for an upcoming Shadows of Brimstone campaign, combining the Forbidden Fortress and Blasted Wastes sets into a thematic East-meets-post-apocalypse mashup.

The creative logic: Japan is the one post-apocalyptic nation on Earth, so connecting the atomic wasteland of the Blasted Wastes to the Forbidden Fortress through darkstone portals has a certain poetic resonance—think kaiju spawned from radiation, samurai wandering irradiated canyons. Daniel walks through his meticulously curated card box, showing custom threat decks mixing Forbidden Fortress enemies with Blasted Wastes creatures, werewolves, and sand crabs. He advocates strongly for buying piecemeal rather than backing massive Kickstarter pledges, and passionately defends the marriage of Eastern and Western themes—citing Seven Samurai and The Magnificent Seven, Yojimbo and A Fistful of Dollars as proof the cowboy and samurai are opposite sides of the same coin.

The bulk of the video is a loving inventory of tiles, tokens, card decks, and storage solutions, revealing just how much organisational work goes into maintaining a Shadows of Brimstone collection. Multiple storage tubs, laminated town sheets, and a Crown Royal bag for dice—the lifestyle game demands a lifestyle commitment.

----

Have you ever combined two seemingly incompatible settings in a game and found unexpected thematic harmony?
